# Interviews

Three reviewers, each asked how they currently decide a screen is "done".

## R1 — a senior reviewer

> I approve things in Slack from a screenshot. Half the time the real page is broken and I never
> see it. The screenshot lied to me.

Recognition and hesitation both matter: the moment of "wait, that's not what I approved" is the
signal worth designing for.

## R2 — a new hire

> It took me a week to understand why we chose the split layout. The reasoning was in someone's
> head, not on the screen.

## R3 — the lead

Wants the whole flow legible at a glance, including the parts that were deliberately skipped.
